message:
🌱 Add ollama: 127.0.0.1:11434: bind: address already in use mission (#978)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,72 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "ollama-707-127-0-0-1-11434-bind-address-already-in-use",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "ollama: 127.0.0.1:11434: bind: address already in use",
+    "description": "127.0.0.1:11434: bind: address already in use. Requested by 59+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check Ollama setup",
+        "description": "Verify your Ollama version:\n```bash\nollama version\n```\nThis issue occurs when `ollama serve` reports \"bind: address already in use\" on port 11434."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Identify the cause",
+        "description": "Ollama automatically starts a background server on first install. Running `ollama serve` manually conflicts with the already-running instance.\n\nCheck what is using port 11434:\n```bash\nlsof -i :11434\n```\nIf you see an existing Ollama process, the server is already running."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Resolve the port conflict",
+        "description": "Option 1: Skip `ollama serve` entirely - the server is already running. Just use:\n```bash\nollama run llama3\n```\n\nOption 2: Stop the existing server and restart manually:\n```bash\n# On macOS\nlaunchctl stop com.ollama.ollama\n\n# On Linux (systemd)\nsystemctl stop ollama\n\n# Then start manually\nollama serve\n```\n\nOption 3: Use a different port:\n```bash\nOLLAMA_HOST=127.0.0.1:11435 ollama serve\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm the issue is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify Ollama is running and accessible:\n```bash\nollama list\n```\nIf you can list models without errors, the server is working correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The \"bind: address already in use\" error occurs because Ollama auto-starts a server on install. Users can skip running `ollama serve`, stop the existing instance first, or use a different port via OLLAMA_HOST.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
